entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: The crop species Arachis hypogaea L. studied for iron deficiency chlorosis resistance.

entities.name: IRON DEFICIENCY CHLOROSIS
entities.description: A physiological disorder characterized by yellowing of leaves due to iron deficiency, often induced by calcareous soils.

entities.name: POT EXPERIMENT
entities.description: A controlled experimental setup using pots with factorial design to test genotypes under different soil conditions.

entities.name: NORMAL SOIL
entities.description: Soil used as a control in the experiment to compare with calcareous soil.

entities.name: CALCAREOUS SOIL
entities.description: Soil rich in calcium carbonate that typically induces iron deficiency chlorosis.

entities.name: VCR
entities.description: Visual Chlorosis Rating, a trait used to measure the severity of iron deficiency chlorosis.

entities.name: SCMR
entities.description: A trait measured to assess the response of groundnut genotypes to iron deficiency chlorosis.

entities.name: CHLOROPHYLL A
entities.description: A primary photosynthetic pigment measured in groundnut leaves.

entities.name: CHLOROPHYLL B
entities.description: An accessory photosynthetic pigment measured in groundnut leaves.

entities.name: TOTAL CHLOROPHYLL
entities.description: The combined amount of chlorophyll a and b in the leaves.

entities.name: ACTIVE IRON CONTENT
entities.description: The concentration of available iron in the leaf tissue.

entities.name: PEROXIDASE ACTIVITY
entities.description: The specific activity of the peroxidase enzyme in the leaves, linked to IDC resistance.

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The total agricultural output of the groundnut genotypes.

entities.name: YIELD COMPONENTS
entities.description: Specific measurable factors that contribute to the overall yield of the crop.
